ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the tweet */,
  "user_screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter Web Client) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was created */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Count of retweets for the tweet */,
  "favorite_count" INTEGER /* Count of favorites for the tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "in_reply_to_screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user this tweet replies to (if any) */,
  "in_reply_to_status_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet being replied to (if any) */,
  "retweeted_status_id" TEXT /* ID of the original tweet being retweeted (if any) */,
  "retweeted_status_user_id" TEXT /* User ID of the original tweet's author (if any) */,
  "retweeted_status_user_screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the original tweet's author (if any) */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical representation of the sentiment analysis of the tweet */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Categorized topic of the tweet */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* Geographical location from where the tweet was posted */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of hashtags used in the tweet */,
  "mentions" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of user mentions in the tweet */,
  "media_urls" TEXT /* URLs of any media attached to the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "user_screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "follower_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the user follows */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified */,
  "profile_description" TEXT /* Description from the user's profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the analytics record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the associated tweet */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the analytics record */,
  "total_retweets" INTEGER /* Total retweets on the date */,
  "total_favorites" INTEGER /* Total favorites on the date */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Calculated engagement rate */,
  "sentiment_trend" REAL /* Trend of sentiment over time */,
  "interaction_count" INTEGER /* Total interactions (retweets + favorites + replies) */,
  "reach_estimate" INTEGER /* Estimated reach of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access record */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Detailed access level (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., Twitter Web Client) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the reply record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the original tweet */,
  "reply_tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the reply tweet */,
  "reply_text" TEXT /* Content of the reply tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_reply_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the retweet record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the original tweet */,
  "retweeted_tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the retweeted tweet */,
  "retweet_text" TEXT /* Content of the retweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_retweeted_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweeted_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
